hints.text: Plate long white + single blue strip left
**Romanian** (Romance, similar to Italian): special letters **Ă Ș Ț** (+ Â, Î)
**Holey poles** with holes reaching the bottom (wide or tall) — Poland's stop short, Hungary's are smaller; round concrete poles too
**Extremely large yellow pole stickers** (town name written in the middle) are specific to Romania
**Waystones** common: large w/ red or blue framing + road number, or rugged actual stone
Outer lines = **short dashes, narrow spaces**; middle lines noticeably **thick** (vs Bulgaria's thin lines)
Directional signs blue, arrow-shaped, white border, road number in red or blue
Town-entry sign = yellow w/ speed limit + a **city skyline**, white name sign below; black sign backs
Chevrons **red-on-white with a yellow frame** (yellow frame most common in Romania)
Bollards thin white w/ a **red vertical strip near the top** (rare; Turkey similar); unique thick-red-border yield sign
Architecture: pastel stone single-family houses, orange-tile or grey-metal roofs, sometimes painted square-tile walls
Tall forested mountains centre/north, flat S/W/E; distinctive **haystack w/ a stick through it**; **Dacia** = >25% of cars
**Hungarian bilingual signs** → Covasna/Harghita/Mureș (centre); orange-tile roofs = west, metal roofs = east; **Maramureș** = decorated wooden gates
